file_analyses.file_purpose: UI metadata file containing comprehensive element definitions for the HP Instant Ink enrollment page. Captures all interactive elements on the enrollment review page including plan cards, shipping information, billing details, and action buttons with their locators, attributes, and properties. Used by the test automation framework to identify and interact with UI elements during enrollment flow testing.

page_info.title: HP Instant Ink – Ink Monthly Subscription | HP Official Site

Button to edit billing and payment information
Primary action button to continue with enrollment after reviewing plan, shipping, and billing details. Disabled until all required information is provided.
Button to add billing information when no payment method is configured
Link to open promo code or PIN code entry dialog for applying discounts or special offers
Tooltip information icon for billing card providing additional help or context
Link to claim special enrollment offer or promotional code
Link to view detailed benefits information about the Instant Ink subscription

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This JSON file contains UI metadata for the HP Instant Ink enrollment review page. It captures 12 interactive elements including plan display cards, edit buttons for plan/shipping/billing, the main continue button, add billing button, promo code entry link, and claim offer link. All primary action elements use data-testid attributes making them highly reliable for test automation. The continue button is initially disabled until all required information (especially billing) is provided. This metadata file is used by the AI-driven test automation framework to dynamically identify and interact with enrollment page elements without hardcoded page objects.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive UI metadata file for the HP Instant Ink home/landing page and account dashboard. Contains detailed element definitions for navigation menu items, account management buttons, cancellation flow elements, plan selection buttons, cookie consent controls, footer links, and various informational links. Provides complete locator information including CSS selectors, XPath expressions, data-testid attributes, and element properties for all interactive components on the home page. Used by the test automation framework to dynamically locate and interact with UI elements during home page and dashboard testing scenarios.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This comprehensive JSON metadata file captures 30+ interactive elements from the HP Instant Ink home page and dashboard. It includes critical elements for testing subscription management flows including cancellation (cancel-your-plan, confirm-cancellation, keep-instant-ink-subscription), plan selection (i_ink-plan-type-card-select-button), cookie consent (onetrust-accept-btn-handler, onetrust-pc-btn-handler), and navigation elements. Many elements use data-testid attributes for reliable identification. The file also includes extensive footer links for privacy, terms, and accessibility. This metadata enables AI-driven test automation to dynamically interact with the home page without hardcoded page objects. The extraction_stats section indicates 30 total elements with 1 auth-related element and all selectors validated.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Main configuration file for the AURA test automation framework. Defines all critical test execution parameters including locale settings, login credentials, environment URLs, wait times, printer configurations for different test scenarios, LLM/AI integration settings for Azure OpenAI, output directories for screenshots and metadata, and optional hints for AI-driven element identification. This file serves as the central configuration hub that controls test behavior across different environments and test scenarios.

locale.description: Locale settings for test execution including country and language

login.description: Login configuration with primary and backup URLs, and placeholder credentials

log_file.description: Path to log file for test execution logs, empty means default location

tmp_folder.description: Temporary folder path for test artifacts

common_test_components.description: Path to common reusable test components directory

large_wait.description: Large wait time in minutes, expected to be greater than 15 minutes for long-running operations

metadata.description: Paths to metadata files including UI controls definitions and reference images

printers.description: Printer configurations for PIE and Stage environments with different subscription states

llm.description: LLM configuration for AI-driven test automation using Azure OpenAI GPT-4o model

output.description: Output directories for test artifacts including screenshots, DOM captures, and metadata files

hints.description: Optional hints to help LLM identify UI elements during AI-driven test execution

schema.description: Path to schema file for validation

ai_enabled.description: Flag to enable or disable AI-driven test automation features

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This YAML configuration file is the central control point for the AURA framework. It defines two printer environments (pie and stage) with multiple printer configurations representing different subscription states (subscribed, unsubscribed, out_of_trial_period, initiate_unsubscribe, subscribed_no_pens). The LLM section configures Azure OpenAI integration for AI-driven element identification and test generation. The hints section provides optional guidance to the AI for identifying login elements. The large_wait of 16 minutes suggests the framework handles long-running test scenarios. The ai_enabled flag controls whether AI features are active. Credentials are placeholders indicating they should be provided via environment variables or secure configuration.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Configuration loader helper module that provides utility functions for loading and parsing the YAML configuration file and extracting printer configurations based on specific criteria. Contains functions to load the main config.yaml file and filter printer definitions by description attribute, supporting test data selection for different test scenarios.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML configuration file, defaults to config.yaml in current directory

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML configuration file from the specified path. Opens the file in read mode using a context manager with open(path, 'r'). Uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary structure. Returns the parsed configuration dictionary containing all settings from the YAML file. If the file does not exist or cannot be parsed, the function will raise an exception. This is the primary entry point for loading framework configuration.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Configuration dictionary loaded from YAML file containing printer definitions

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description filter to match against printer configurations (e.g., 'subscribed', 'unsubscribed', 'out_of_trial_period')

functions.what_it_does: Filters and returns printer configurations from the config dictionary that match the specified description. First extracts the 'printers' list from the config using config.get('printers', []). Then searches for the 'pie' key within the printers list using next() with a generator expression. Handles two cases: if pie_printers is a single dictionary, checks if its description matches and returns a list with one printer dict containing model and serial_number. If pie_printers is a list of dictionaries, uses list comprehension to filter all printers where the description attribute matches the provided description parameter, returning a list of dicts with model and serial_number for each match. Returns an empty list if no printers are found or if the pie key does not exist. This function enables test data selection based on printer subscription state.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides two essential utility functions for configuration management. The load_config function is straightforward and loads YAML configuration using the safe_load method which prevents arbitrary code execution. The get_printers_by_description function is more complex and handles both single printer and multiple printer configurations, filtering by description attribute. This enables tests to dynamically select printers based on their subscription state (subscribed, unsubscribed, out_of_trial_period, etc.). The function specifically looks for the 'pie' environment printers, suggesting there may be other environments (like 'stage') that could be accessed with similar logic. The function returns a simplified list of dicts with only model and serial_number, stripping out other printer attributes.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Pytest configuration file (conftest.py) that defines test fixtures, command-line options, and test setup logic for the AURA framework. Configures logging for test execution, provides fixtures for loading and parsing YAML test case files, implements YAML import/expansion logic for modular test definitions, and handles test flow parsing with support for multi-phase test execution. This file is automatically loaded by pytest and makes all fixtures available to test files.

functions.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that configures logging for the entire test session. Automatically runs before any tests due to autouse=True. First removes any existing log handlers that pytest may have already attached by iterating through logging.root.handlers and calling removeHandler() for each. Then configures logging using basicConfig with INFO level, a timestamp format string, and two handlers: FileHandler writing to the log file path specified in config['log_file'], and StreamHandler writing to sys.stdout for console output. This ensures all test logs are captured both to file and console with consistent formatting.
functions.usage_example: # Fixture runs automatically, no explicit call needed

parameters.name: parser
parameters.description: Pytest argument parser for adding custom command-line options

functions.docstring: This hook adds a new command-line option for specifying the YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Pytest hook function that adds a custom command-line option --yaml-file to the pytest command. Uses parser.addoption() to register the option with action='store' to capture the value, default=None meaning the option is optional, and a help string describing the option's purpose. This allows test execution to be parameterized with different YAML test case files by running pytest with --yaml-file path/to/test.yaml. The option value can be accessed in fixtures using request.config.getoption('--yaml-file').

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML file to load

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML file from the specified file path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding using a context manager. Uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary. Returns the parsed dictionary, or an empty dict if the file is empty. Includes error handling: raises FileNotFoundError with a descriptive message if the file does not exist, and raises ValueError with the YAML parsing error details if the file contains invalid YAML syntax. This function is used internally by other functions to load both main test YAML files and imported component files.

functions.docstring: If the YAML contains flow/steps, return that value. Otherwise return the whole document.
functions.what_it_does: Extracts the test flow steps from a parsed YAML data structure. Attempts to access data['flow']['steps'] using nested dictionary access. If successful, returns the steps list. If a KeyError or TypeError occurs (meaning the flow or steps keys do not exist or data is not a dict), catches the exception and returns the entire data structure unchanged. This allows the function to handle both YAML files with a structured flow/steps hierarchy and simpler YAML files that contain steps directly at the root level. Used during YAML import expansion to extract the relevant test steps from imported component files.

parameters.name: base_path
parameters.description: Base directory path for resolving relative file imports

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: YAML node to process, can be list, dict, or scalar value

functions.docstring: Recursively expand any list items containing a 'file' key by loading the referenced YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Recursively processes a YAML data structure to expand file imports. Handles three cases: If node is a list, iterates through each item. For items that are dicts containing a 'file' key, constructs the full file path by joining config['common_test_components'] with the file value, prints the file path for debugging, loads the referenced YAML file using load_yaml_file(), extracts its flow steps using extract_flow_steps(), and extends the expanded list with those steps. For other list items, recursively calls expand_imports and appends the result. If node is a dict, returns a new dict with expand_imports called recursively on each value. If node is a scalar (string, number, etc.), returns it unchanged. This enables modular test definitions where common test components can be defined in separate YAML files and imported into main test flows using {file: 'component.yaml'} syntax.

parameters.name: config_path
parameters.description: Path to the main test flow YAML configuration file

functions.what_it_does: Parses a test flow YAML file with support for multi-phase test execution and file imports. First determines the base directory of the config file using os.path.dirname and os.path.abspath. Loads the root YAML file using load_yaml_file(). Extracts the phases dictionary from root.get('flow', {}).get('phases', {}). Initializes an empty all_steps list. Iterates through all phase values in the phases dict. For each phase's steps list (if not None), calls expand_imports() to recursively expand any file imports, then extends all_steps with the expanded steps. Returns a dictionary with structure {'flow': {'steps': all_steps}} containing all steps from all phases flattened into a single list with imports expanded. This enables complex test flows organized into phases (setup, execution, teardown) with reusable components imported from separate files.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Pytest request object providing access to test context and configuration

functions.docstring: This fixture reads the YAML file specified by the --yaml-file option and returns its content as a Python dictionary.
functions.what_it_does: Pytest fixture that provides parsed YAML test data to test functions. Retrieves the --yaml-file command-line option value using request.config.getoption('--yaml-file'). If no YAML file was provided, calls pytest.skip() to skip the test with an explanatory message. If a file path was provided, calls parse_flow() to load and parse the YAML file with import expansion and phase flattening. Returns the parsed flow dictionary containing all test steps. Includes error handling: if the file is not found, calls pytest.fail() with a descriptive message. If YAML parsing fails, calls pytest.fail() with the error details. Tests can use this fixture by declaring it as a parameter, and the fixture will provide the complete test flow data structure.

parameters.name: pytestconfig
parameters.description: Pytest configuration object providing access to command-line options

functions.docstring: Session-scoped fixture that returns the file path passed with --yaml-file.
functions.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that provides the YAML file path to tests. Retrieves the yaml_file option value from pytestconfig using getoption('yaml_file'). Note this uses 'yaml_file' without dashes, which is the internal option name. If no path was provided (value is None or empty), calls pytest.skip() to skip the test with message '--yaml-file was not provided'. Otherwise returns the file path string. Being session-scoped means this fixture is evaluated once per test session and the same value is reused across all tests, improving performance. Tests can use this fixture to access the raw file path without parsing the YAML content.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This conftest.py file is a sophisticated pytest configuration that enables data-driven testing with YAML test case definitions. The key innovation is the expand_imports function which allows modular test composition - common test steps can be defined once in separate YAML files and imported into multiple test flows using {file: 'component.yaml'} syntax. The parse_flow function supports multi-phase test organization (setup, execution, teardown phases) and flattens them into a single step list. The yaml_data fixture is the primary entry point for tests to access parsed test data. The configure_logging fixture ensures consistent logging across all tests. The framework loads config.yaml at module level making configuration globally available. This architecture supports the AI-driven test automation approach where test cases are defined declaratively in YAML and executed by a generic test runner.

key_reusable_methods: load_config — Loads YAML configuration file and returns parsed dictionary
get_printers_by_description — Filters printer configurations by description attribute for test data selection
configure_logging — Session fixture that configures logging to file and console
pytest_addoption — Adds --yaml-file command-line option to pytest
load_yaml_file — Loads and parses YAML file with error handling
extract_flow_steps — Extracts flow steps from YAML data structure
expand_imports — Recursively expands file imports in YAML test definitions
parse_flow — Parses multi-phase test flow YAML with import expansion
yaml_data — Fixture providing parsed YAML test data to test functions
yaml_file_path — Session fixture providing YAML file path
key_reusable_locators: Enroll.json: [data-testid='plan-card'] — Plan display card on enrollment page
